rulings.Case Title: CHARLES ENGELBERG VS. ELLEN TSANG, AS TRUSTEE OF THE ARZA TRUST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Hearing To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday January 15, 2015, line 1. PLAINTIFF CHARLES ENGELBERG Motion And Notice Of Hearing To Tax Costs Plaintiff's Motion to Tax Costs and Sanctions is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Plaintiff's motion to tax costs is granted. Defendant is not entitled to recover costs incurred in defending the complaint (such as filing fee for answer) per agreement of the parties evidenced by written communications; and Defendant's claims for costs incurred in prosecuting the cross-complaint is pre-mature. Plaintiff's request for sanctions under CCP 128.5 is denied. Plaintiff failed to show compliance with CCP 128.5(f). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL DOUGLAS ET AL VS. DAHLIA HOTEL, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday January 15, 2015, line 3. DEFENDANT DAHLIA HOTEL, LLC, SANJAY PATEL, VIJAY PATEL AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. The applicable statute of limitations as to the cause of action for SFRO violation is 1 year; the applicable statute of limitations as to all other causes of action is 2 years. To the extent that some of the complained about acts occurred outside the applicable statute of limitations and some within the statute of limitations as to each particular Plaintiff, the demurrer as to the whole cause of action/and complaint is overruled. (1) The demurrer to the cause of action for SFRO violation is sustained without leave to amend as to all Plaintiffs who vacated the premises 1 year before the filing of the complaint. (2) The demurer as to all other causes of action is sustained without leave to amend as to all Plaintiffs who vacated the premises 2 years before the filing of the complaint. (3) The demurrer as to the cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress cause of action is overruled. All elements of this cause of action are sufficiently alleged. (4) The demurer to the cause of action for injunctive relief is sustained without leave to amend as to all Plaintiffs who do not currently reside at the premises. No substantive opposition filed. Defendant is ordered to prepare a proposed order outlining the ruling as to each Plaintiff in accordance with this Court's general ruling as specified above. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: VISHNU SHAH VS. JOHN TELLEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Deft And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft (Ccp Sec 1170.8, 2023.010, 2030.290, 2031.300, 2033.280(B) And (C)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday January 15, 2015, line 8. PLAINTIFF VISHNU SHAH Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Deft And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft (Ccp Sec 1170.8, 2023.010, 2030.290, 2031.300, 2033.280(B) And (C) GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant shall provide complete verified responses without objections to Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories and Requests for Production within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Admissions are deemed admitted. Sanctions granted in the amount of $1,110. = (501/REQ)
